title: rosa agrestis subvar. spathulifolia
intro: Rosa agrestis subvar. spathulifolia, also known as the wild rose, is a variant of the Rosa agrestis species that is characterized by its spatula-shaped leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Rosa agrestis subvar. spathulifolia are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and fever.
